title: lessingia filaginifolia var. californica
intro: Lessingia filaginifolia var. californica, also known as California sea lavender, is a flowering plant species native to the coastal regions of California. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and congestion, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of California sea lavender include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate coughs, and soothe respiratory tract irritations. California sea lavender can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried herb for medicinal purposes, and its essential oil can also be used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and ease respiratory discomfort.
